docs.title: Remitpro 🎓
docs.text:
The Ultimate Multi-tenant SaaS for School Fees Management.

Remitpro is a production-ready, enterprise-grade Fintech solution for educational institutions. Built for the Nigerian market, it automates fee collection through Monnify virtual accounts and features a unique "N200 Flat-Fee" revenue model for the platform owner.

docs.title: 📚 Documentation
docs.text:
• User Guide: A complete walkthrough for School Admins & Bursars.
  • Technical Manual: Deep-dive into architecture, RLS, and Fintech logic.
  • Deployment Guide: Steps to launch Remitpro in a production environment.
  • Super-Admin Guide: Documentation for the SaaS platform owner.

docs.title: 🏢 Enterprise Support Documents
docs.text:
• Security & Compliance: RLS, data privacy, and fintech safety frameworks.
  • API Reference: Documentation for backend microservices and external integrations.
  • Service Level Agreement (SLA): Uptime, support, and settlement commitments.
  • Product Roadmap: Future features and planned platform expansions.
  • Manual Test Guide: Task-based QA and testing instructions.

docs.title: ⚡ Enterprise Features
docs.text:
• Multi-tenant SaaS Architecture: Host multiple schools on a single platform with strict logical isolation using PostgreSQL Row Level Security (RLS).
  • Automated Fee Splitting (The ₦200 Rule):
    • Every transaction automatically deducts a flat ₦200 platform fee.
    • Funds are instantly routed: Platform Owner (₦200) vs School Subaccount (Remainder).
  • Fintech Ecosystem (Parent Wallet):
    • Parents fund an internal wallet via Monnify.
    • Real-time settlement of fees, store items, and transport from wallet balances.
  • Smart Attendance & Biometrics:
    • Geofencing: Staff must be within the school perimeter (GPS) to clock in.
    • Facial Verification: Multi-step liveness check (Blink/Smile) using react-webcam.
    • Offline Student Roll-call: Teachers can mark attendance without internet; data syncs automatically in the background.
  • Academic Intelligence Suite:
    • Grading & Assessment: Bulk score uploads from Excel, automated rank calculation, and departmental analytics.
    • Curriculum Repository: Centralized, version-controlled repository for Lesson Plans and Schemes of Work with administrative approval workflows.
    • Computer Based Testing (CBT): Full quiz builder for teachers and timed exam mode for students.
  • Operational Efficiency:
    • Centralized Broadcast Center: Multi-channel (SMS/Email) announcements to parents, staff, or specific classes.
    • Staff Appraisals: Formalized termly performance reviews and objective tracking.
    • Automated ID Card Engine: One-click generation of professional PVC-format vertical ID cards for students and staff.
  • Super-Admin Control Center: A dedicated dashboard for the SaaS owner to monitor global revenue splits, school health, and platform-wide student data.

docs.title: 🛠️ Technology Stack
docs.text:
• Frontend: React 18 + Vite
  • Styling: Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui (Radix Primitives)
  • Backend microservices: Node.js/Express (TypeScript) server (located in server/)
  • Database Architecture: Neon PostgreSQL + Supabase (Auth + Storage)
  • ORM: Drizzle ORM for migrations and type-safe auditing.
  • Fintech Integration: Monnify API (Subaccounts, Reserved Accounts, Webhooks).
  • State Management: TanStack Query v5.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Node.js (v20+ recommended)
  • Neon PostgreSQL Account
  • Supabase Account
  • Monnify API Keys (Merchant Code, API Key, Secret Key)

docs.title: 1. Installation
docs.text:
git clone <repository_url>
cd remitpro
npm install

docs.title: 2. Database Setup
docs.text:
Remitpro uses Drizzle ORM to manage its high-integrity schema. Ensure you configure your DATABASE_URL pointing to your Neon instance in your .env file first.

# Push schema to Neon database
npm run db:push

# Apply multi-tenant security policies (RLS) to tables
npm run db:rls

# Setup system triggers (Auto-profile creation)
npm run db:triggers

docs.title: 3. Environment Variables
docs.text:
Create a .env file in the root directory:

# Client Supabase Credentials
VITE_SUPABASE_URL=your_project_url
VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=your_anon_key

# Backend Microservices URL
VITE_MICROSERVICES_URL=http://localhost:5000/api

# Database connection
DATABASE_URL=your_neon_postgres_connection_string

# Backend Credentials
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=your_supabase_service_role_key
MONNIFY_API_KEY=your_monnify_api_key
MONNIFY_SECRET_KEY=your_monnify_secret_key
MONNIFY_BASE_URL=https://sandbox.monnify.com
PLATFORM_SUBACCOUNT_CODE=your_fees_subaccount_code
RESEND_API_KEY=your_resend_api_key
TERMII_API_KEY=your_termii_api_key

docs.title: 4. Run the Express Backend Server
docs.text:
To start the backend microservices server locally:

# Run the Express server in watch/development mode
npm run server:dev

Then run the React frontend:

# Start Vite development server
npm run dev

docs.title: 👮 Roles & Security
docs.text: Role Permissions Super-Admin Global platform oversight, view total revenue (N200/tx), manage all schools. School Admin Manage their specific school settings, staff, students, and billing. Bursar View students, manage debt lists, and record manual payments.

docs.title: Enforcing Isolation
docs.text: We use logical isolation at the DB level. Every query is scoped via: ALTER TABLE ... ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; CREATE POLICY ... USING (school_id = get_auth_school_id());

docs.title: 📖 Usage Guide
docs.text:
1. Sign Up: Create an account via the standard login/signup page.
  2. Onboarding: After signup, you will be redirected to the Onboarding Page. Enter your school details and Settlement Bank Account.
  3. Wait for Provisioning: The backend automatically calls Monnify to create your school Subaccount.
  4. Enroll Students: Import your student list via CSV.
  5. Collect Fees: Click "Generate Virtual Account" for a student. Parents can then transfer funds directly to that account.

docs.title: API Reference & Integration Guide 🔌
docs.text: Remitpro provides a suite of internal and external API endpoints via a Node.js/Express server to power the multi-tenant school operations, payment services, and messaging workflows.

docs.title: 🏗️ Base Configuration
docs.text:
• Local Development URL: http://localhost:5001/api
  • Production URL: https://[YOUR_EXPRESS_HOST]/api
  • Authentication: Custom JWT Auth Token (sent via Authorization: Bearer <JWT>). User login, registration, and sessions are completely database-driven and managed by the Express microservices.

docs.title: 1. Register School & Owner (POST /api/auth/signup)
docs.text:
Registers a new school tenant and creates the default owner account in the profiles table. - Method: POST - Payload: 

{
  "email": "owner@school.com",
  "password": "SecurePassword123!",
  "fullName": "Adebayo Adesina",
  "schoolName": "Lagos Academic Center",
  "phone": "+2348031112222",
  "address": "12 Broad St, Lagos"
}
- Response: 200 OK 
{
  "success": true,
  "token": "eyJhbGciOiJI...",
  "user": {
    "id": "uuid-string-here",
    "email": "owner@school.com",
    "fullName": "Adebayo Adesina",
    "role": "owner",
    "schoolId": "uuid-school-id"
  }
}

docs.title: 2. User Login (POST /api/auth/login)
docs.text:
Authenticates user credentials and issues a custom JWT access token. - Method: POST - Payload: 

{
  "email": "user@school.com",
  "password": "SecurePassword123!"
}
- Response: 200 OK (returns success, token, and user profile data).

docs.title: 3. Get Active Session (GET /api/auth/session)
docs.text: Retrieves the logged-in user profile from the database based on the provided JWT bearer token. - Method: GET - Headers: Authorization: Bearer <JWT> - Response: 200 OK (returns the user profile data).

docs.title: 4. Accept User Invitation (POST /api/auth/accept-invite)
docs.text:
Completes registration for a new user by verifying their invitation JWT token and saving their password credentials. - Method: POST - Payload: 

{
  "token": "invitation-jwt-token-here",
  "password": "UserChosenPassword123!",
  "fullName": "Chioma Nze"
}
- Response: 200 OK (returns session token and user profile data).

docs.title: 5. Onboard School (POST /api/onboard-school)
docs.text:
Initializes a new school workspace, provisions their tenant record in Neon, and sets up a Monnify bank sub-account for split remittances. - Method: POST - Headers: Authorization: Bearer <Admin_JWT> - Payload: 

{
  "name": "Greenwood International School",
  "email": "admin@greenwood.edu.ng",
  "phone": "+2348031234567",
  "address": "12 High School Rd, Lagos",
  "bankName": "Wema Bank",
  "accountNumber": "0123456789",
  "accountName": "Greenwood Edu Services"
}
- Response: 200 OK 
{
  "success": true,
  "schoolId": "uuid-string-here",
  "subAccountCode": "MFY_SUB_..."
}

docs.title: 6. Generate Virtual Account (POST /api/generate-virtual-account)
docs.text:
Provisions a dedicated bank virtual account (Wema/Rolez) for a student via the Monnify API. - Method: POST - Headers: Authorization: Bearer <User_JWT> - Payload: 

{
  "studentId": "uuid-student-id",
  "schoolId": "uuid-school-id"
}
- Response: 200 OK returning details of the reserved bank account.

docs.title: 7. Invite Staff User (POST /api/invite-user)
docs.text:
Initiates the onboarding/RBAC invite flow for bursars and other staff members by issuing a secure JWT-based invitation token. - Method: POST - Headers: Authorization: Bearer <Admin_JWT> - Payload: 

{
  "email": "bursar@greenwood.edu.ng",
  "role": "bursar",
  "schoolId": "uuid-school-id"
}
- Response: 200 OK (invitation link generated and sent to the staff member).

docs.title: 8. Verify Monnify Payment (POST /api/verify-payment)
docs.text:
Performs a direct server-to-server transaction verification against the Monnify API and processes the student ledger sync. - Method: POST - Headers: Authorization: Bearer <User_JWT> - Payload: 

{
  "transactionReference": "SP-TX-1004321"
}
- Response: 200 OK with verified payment confirmation details.

docs.title: 9. Monnify Webhook Handler (POST /api/monnify-webhook)
docs.text: Processes transaction notifications securely received from Monnify's transaction servers. - Method: POST - Headers: monnify-signature (HMAC-SHA512 checksum validating the payload against the merchant secret) - Payload: Standard Monnify webhook JSON notification payload containing transactionReference, amountPaid, paymentStatus, etc. - Response: 200 OK (Processes splits, sets student ledger status to Paid, and emits system alerts).

docs.title: 10. Promote Students (POST /api/promote-students)
docs.text:
Performs batch promotion of students to the next class levels and rolls over the term cycle. - Method: POST - Headers: Authorization: Bearer <Admin_JWT> - Payload: 

{
  "schoolId": "uuid-school-id",
  "promotions": [
    { "fromClass": "JSS1", "toClass": "JSS2" },
    { "fromClass": "JSS2", "toClass": "JSS3" }
  ]
}
- Response: 200 OK (Returns summary of promoted student counts).

docs.title: 11. Send Proxy Email (POST /api/send-email)
docs.text:
Enables transactional mailing (via Resend) without exposing server API keys to frontend assets. - Method: POST - Payload: 

{
  "to": "recipient@domain.com",
  "subject": "Greenwood Onboarding Invite",
  "html": "<p>Invite body...</p>"
}
- Response: 200 OK

docs.title: 12. Send Proxy SMS (POST /api/send-sms)
docs.text:
Enables transactional mobile SMS sending (via Termii) to parents and staff. - Method: POST - Payload: 

{
  "to": "+2348000000000",
  "message": "Dear Parent, fee payment of ₦50,000 was successfully processed."
}
- Response: 200 OK

docs.title: 📡 Webhook/Realtime Specifications
docs.text: If you wish to subscribe to payment events asynchronously, configure listener endpoints against Supabase Realtime listening on the transactions table.

docs.title: Event Schema: PAYMENT_SUCCESS
docs.text:
Emitted automatically when a virtual account payment settles. 

{
  "event": "PAYMENT_SUCCESS",
  "data": {
    "student_id": "uuid-student-id",
    "amount": 48500.00,
    "transaction_ref": "MNFY-98217340",
    "paid_at": "2026-06-20T12:00:00Z"
  }
}

docs.text:
• Standard Tier: 120 requests / minute per tenant.
  • Enterprise Tier: Scalable clusters with custom throughput settings.

docs.title: 1. Executive Summary
docs.text: Remitpro is a multi-tenant SaaS platform designed for Nigerian educational institutions to manage finances, academics, HR, and operations. Built on a modular service architecture, the system leverages React (Vite) on the frontend, a Node.js/Express backend (microservices), and Neon (PostgreSQL) managed via Drizzle ORM to deliver a secure, scalable, and offline-capable solution.

docs.title: 1.1 Key Architectural Characteristics
docs.text: Characteristic Implementation Multi-Tenancy Row-Level Security (RLS) with school_id isolation Security Model Zero-trust with client-side RLS + server-side validation Data Integrity Immutable financial logs, audit trails, ACID compliance Offline Support PWA with Service Workers + IndexedDB sync queue Scalability Modular microservices, Neon connection pooling, indexed queries Compliance Nigerian financial regulations (WHT, Pension, TRCN)

docs.title: 2. Architectural Principles
docs.text: The system is built on the following core principles:

docs.title: 2.1 Design Principles
docs.text:
1. Security by Default: All data access controlled by RLS; sensitive operations in Express Microservices Backend
  2. Offline-First: Critical workflows (attendance) function without internet connectivity
  3. Financial Integrity: Immutable transaction logs; double-entry accounting principles
  4. Type Safety: End-to-end TypeScript with specific types for all service methods and component props. Avoidance of the any keyword in favor of strict typing and unknown error handling.
  5. Multi-Tenant Isolation: Strict data segregation at database level
  6. Auditability: Comprehensive logging of all state-changing operations
  7. Progressive Enhancement: Core features work on basic browsers; enhanced on modern ones

docs.title: 2.2 Architectural Patterns
docs.text:
• Client-First Architecture: Direct database access from frontend (secured by RLS)
  • CQRS-Lite: Separate read/write paths for complex financial operations
  • Event-Driven: Webhooks and database triggers for async workflows
  • Service Layer Pattern: Business logic encapsulated in TypeScript services
  • Repository Pattern: Drizzle ORM abstracts database operations

docs.title: 5.1 Express Microservices Backend
docs.text: The backend business logic and secure API integrations run on a modular Node.js/Express server (located in server/) written in TypeScript.

docs.title: Client Request Interception
docs.text: To maintain compatibility and prevent client refactoring, all supabase.functions.invoke(...) calls from the React frontend are dynamically intercepted in client.ts and rerouted to the Express server gateway (http://localhost:5000/api locally, or VITE_MICROSERVICES_URL in production). Active session JWT tokens are forwarded in the Authorization header so the server can securely verify caller identities.

docs.title: Endpoints / Route Handlers
docs.text:
The Express server registers 10 separate routes corresponding to the migrated functions. These routes query the Neon PostgreSQL database directly using Drizzle ORM instead of going through PostgREST/Supabase client:

Route Path HTTP Method Authentication Purpose /api/onboard-school POST Service Role / Admin JWT Create school + provision Monnify subaccount in Neon DB /api/invite-user POST Admin JWT Elevate staff role, create DB profile, send Resend invite link /api/generate-virtual-account POST User JWT (Fallback) Create student reserved Monnify account & store details /api/monnify-webhook POST Webhook Signature Process payment notification & mark ledger paid /api/webhook-payment POST Webhook Signature Alternate route for webhook validation /api/verify-payment POST User JWT Server-to-server Monnify transaction check and ledger sync /api/promote-students POST Admin JWT Run academic term rollover and promote classes in Neon /api/send-email POST Service Role Proxy route to send email using Resend API /api/send-sms POST Service Role Proxy route to send SMS notifications using Termii API /api/monnify-create-account POST Service Role Base virtual account setup

docs.title: Example: Payment Webhook Security
docs.text:
// server/src/routes/webhookPayment.ts
import { Router } from 'express';
import crypto from 'crypto';

const router = Router();

router.post('/', async (req, res) => {
  const payload = req.body;
  const signature = req.headers['monnify-signature'];

  // Verify webhook authenticity using HMAC-SHA512
  const computedSignature = crypto
    .createHmac('sha512', process.env.MONNIFY_SECRET_KEY || '')
    .update(JSON.stringify(payload))
    .digest('hex');

  if (signature !== computedSignature) {
    return res.status(401).send('Invalid signature');
  }

  // Process payment directly in Neon via Drizzle ORM...
  await db.insert(transactions).values({
    studentId: payload.productCode,
    amount: payload.amountPaid,
    transactionReference: payload.transactionReference,
    // ... audit details
  });

  return res.status(200).send('OK');
});

docs.title: 5.2 Service Layer Pattern
docs.text:
Business logic is encapsulated in 15 TypeScript services:

// Example: FeeService.ts
export class FeeService {
  static async generateLedgerEntries(
    studentId: string,
    classLevel: string,
    term: string
  ): Promise<void> {
    // 1. Fetch master fee schedule for class
    const schedule = await supabase
      .from('master_fee_schedule')
      .select('*')
      .eq('class_level', classLevel);

    // 2. Create ledger entries for student
    const entries = schedule.data.map(fee => ({
      student_id: studentId,
      fee_schedule_id: fee.id,
      fee_name: fee.name,
      amount: fee.amount,
      term: term,
      is_paid: false
    }));

    await supabase.from('student_ledgers').insert(entries);

    // 3. Audit log
    await AuditService.log('fee_generation', 'student', studentId);
  }
}

Service Responsibilities: - AcademicService: Grade calculations, report cards, class positions - FeeService: Ledger generation, payment allocation - PayrollService: Salary calculations, deductions (pension, WHT) - NotificationService: Multi-channel messaging (Email, SMS, In-App) - WalletService: Parent wallet management, balance tracking - AuditService: Compliance logging

    SCHOOLS ||--o{ STUDENTS : contains
    SCHOOLS ||--o{ PROFILES : employs
    SCHOOLS ||--o{ MASTER_FEE_SCHEDULE : defines

    STUDENTS ||--o{ STUDENT_LEDGERS : owes
    STUDENTS ||--o{ TRANSACTIONS : pays
    STUDENTS ||--o{ ACADEMIC_RECORDS : achieves

    MASTER_FEE_SCHEDULE ||--o{ STUDENT_LEDGERS : generates

    PROFILES ||--o{ AUDIT_LOGS : performs

    SCHOOLS {
        uuid id PK
        text name
        text monnify_subaccount_code
        text current_session
        text current_term
        numeric geofence_lat
        numeric geofence_lng
    }

    STUDENTS {
        uuid id PK
        uuid school_id FK
        text student_id UK
        text class
        numeric outstanding_balance
        text virtual_account_number UK
    }

    STUDENT_LEDGERS {
        uuid id PK
        uuid student_id FK
        uuid fee_schedule_id FK
        numeric amount
        numeric amount_settled
        boolean is_paid
        text term
    }

    TRANSACTIONS {
        uuid id PK
        uuid student_id FK
        numeric amount
        text transaction_reference UK
        numeric balance_before
        numeric balance_after
        numeric platform_fee
    }

docs.title: 6.2 Financial Data Model
docs.text:
The financial system uses a modified double-entry approach:

Master Fee Schedule (Template)
        ↓
Student Ledgers (Debt Tracker)
        ↓
Transactions (Immutable Payment Log)

Key Principles: 1. Master Fee Schedule: Single source of truth for fees per class 2. Student Ledgers: Individual debt records (mutable: amount_settled) 3. Transactions: Immutable payment history (never deleted/updated) 4. Balance Calculation: outstanding_balance = SUM(ledger.amount - ledger.amount_settled)

docs.title: 6.3 Indexing Strategy
docs.text:
-- High-traffic query optimization
CREATE INDEX ledger_school_idx ON student_ledgers(school_id);
CREATE INDEX ledger_student_idx ON student_ledgers(student_id);
CREATE INDEX ledger_unpaid_idx ON student_ledgers(school_id, is_paid);
CREATE INDEX transactions_created_idx ON transactions(created_at);
CREATE INDEX students_created_idx ON students(created_at);

-- Unique constraints for data integrity
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX school_id_student_id_idx ON students(school_id, student_id);
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX student_position_idx ON class_positions(student_id, session, term);

docs.title: 6.4 Data Retention & Archival
docs.text: Data Type Retention Archive Strategy Transactions Permanent Never delete (compliance) Audit Logs 7 years Move to cold storage after 2 years Academic Records Permanent Archived per session Attendance 5 years Aggregate to monthly summaries Notifications 90 days Delete after read + 90 days

docs.title: 7.1 Multi-Tenant Isolation (RLS)
docs.text:
Row-Level Security is the cornerstone of data isolation:

-- Example RLS Policy for students table
CREATE POLICY "school_isolation_policy" ON students
  USING (school_id = (
    SELECT school_id FROM profiles WHERE id = auth.uid()
  ));

-- Super-admin bypass
CREATE POLICY "super_admin_access" ON students
  USING (
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM profiles 
      WHERE id = auth.uid() 
      AND role = 'super_admin'
    )
  );

RLS Coverage: - ✅ All tables with school_id have isolation policies - ✅ profiles table links auth.uid() to school_id - ✅ Super-admin role can bypass for platform monitoring - ✅ Policies enforced at PostgreSQL level (not application)

docs.title: 7.2 Authentication & Authorization
docs.text:
graph LR
    A[User Login] --> B{Supabase Auth}
    B -->|Success| C[JWT Token]
    C --> D[Frontend Stores Token]
    D --> E[API Requests Include Token]
    E --> F{RLS Check}
    F -->|Authorized| G[Return Data]
    F -->|Denied| H[403 Forbidden]

Auth Flow: 1. User authenticates via Supabase Auth (email/password) 2. JWT token issued (contains user_id) 3. Token included in all API requests 4. PostgreSQL RLS uses auth.uid() from JWT 5. Data filtered by school_id automatically

Role-Based Access Control (RBAC):

Role Permissions Implementation super_admin Platform-wide access RLS bypass policy admin Full school access Standard RLS teacher Read students, write grades Application-level checks accountant Finance module only Application-level checks parent View own children only Custom RLS on student_id

docs.title: 7.3 Payment Security
docs.text:
Monnify Integration Security: 1. Webhook Signature Validation: HMAC-SHA512 verification 2. Idempotency: transaction_reference unique constraint prevents duplicates 3. Split Settlement: Platform fee deducted at gateway level 4. PCI Compliance: No card data stored (Monnify handles)

Virtual Account Security: - Accounts generated server-side only (/api/generate-virtual-account route) - Account numbers permanently linked to student_id - Cannot be reassigned or deleted

docs.title: 7.4 Data Protection
docs.text: Layer Protection Mechanism Transport TLS 1.3 (HTTPS) Storage PostgreSQL encryption at rest Secrets Environment variables (never committed) API Keys Hidden in Express microservices backend (not exposed to client) Sensitive Fields NIN, TIN encrypted in database Audit Trail All mutations logged to audit_logs

docs.title: 8.3 Integration Resilience
docs.text:
Retry Strategy: 

async function retryWithBackoff(fn, maxRetries = 3) {
  for (let i = 0; i < maxRetries; i++) {
    try {
      return await fn();
    } catch (error) {
      if (i === maxRetries - 1) throw error;
      await new Promise(resolve => 
        setTimeout(resolve, Math.pow(2, i) * 1000)
      );
    }
  }
}

Circuit Breaker: Implemented for external APIs to prevent cascade failures

docs.text: Decision Rationale Neon & Supabase over Firebase PostgreSQL (relational) better for financial data; RLS built-in/managed on Neon. Vite over Create React App 10x faster builds; modern tooling Drizzle over Prisma Lighter weight; better TypeScript inference Shadcn/UI over Material-UI Unstyled primitives; full design control React Query over Redux Server state management; less boilerplate

docs.title: 🚀 Production Deployment Guide: Remitpro
docs.text: This guide outlines the professional workflow for deploying the Remitpro multi-tenant SaaS to a production environment using a Node.js/Express microservices server and a Neon PostgreSQL database.

docs.title: 📋 Prerequisites
docs.text:
• A Neon Account (for PostgreSQL serverless database hosting).
  • A Supabase Account (for Auth/Identity and Object Storage).
  • A Monnify Live Merchant Account (for payment processing and split billing).
  • Node.js 20+ installed locally.
  • A Render, Railway, or Heroku account for backend API server hosting.
  • A Vercel or Netlify account for frontend hosting.

docs.title: 1. Neon Database Setup
docs.text:
1. Create a new project in your Neon Dashboard.
  2. Note your DATABASE_URL connection string (ensure it uses pooled connections -pooler for production scaling).

docs.title: 2. Supabase Project (Auth & Storage)
docs.text:
1. Create a new project in the Supabase Dashboard.
  2. Note your Project URL, API Keys (both anon and service_role).
  3. Enable standard Supabase Auth configurations (site URL, SMTP settings).
  4. Create a bucket named attendance-photos in the Supabase Storage console and set it to public or apply proper RLS.

docs.title: 3. Monnify Live Setup
docs.text:
1. Log in to your Monnify Live Dashboard.
  2. Obtain your Merchant Code, API Key, and Secret Key.
  3. Create a Main Subaccount for platform fees and note the subAccountCode.

docs.title: 🗄️ Step 2: Database & Schema Migration
docs.text: Remitpro uses Drizzle ORM for schema management. Do not run SQL tables scripts manually unless executing structural triggers.

docs.title: 1. Run Schema Migration
docs.text:
Set your production DATABASE_URL in your local environment, then push the schema: 

# Push the schema definitions to Neon PostgreSQL
npm run db:push

docs.title: 2. Apply Security & Rules
docs.text:
Apply the Row-Level Security (RLS) policies and triggers: 

# 1. Apply multi-tenant security policies (RLS)
npm run db:rls

# 2. Setup system triggers (e.g. auth sync profiles and default sessions)
npm run db:triggers

docs.title: 3. Initial Seeding (Optional)
docs.text:
If provisioning default templates, seed data: 

npm run db:seed

docs.title: ⚡ Step 3: Express Backend Deployment
docs.text: The backend server is a Node.js Express application located in server/. You can deploy this to Render, Railway, Fly.io, or any Node-compatible container service.

docs.title: 1. Configure Server Environment Variables
docs.text: On your backend host dashboard (e.g., Render Web Service or Fly.io secrets), set the following environment variables: - DATABASE_URL: Your production Neon PostgreSQL connection string. - SUPABASE_URL: Your production Supabase URL. - SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: Your production Supabase service role API key. - MONNIFY_API_KEY: Your production Monnify API Key. - MONNIFY_SECRET_KEY: Your production Monnify Secret Key. - MONNIFY_BASE_URL: https://api.monnify.com (Live URL) - PLATFORM_SUBACCOUNT_CODE: Your subaccount code for collecting the ₦200 platform fee. - RESEND_API_KEY: Your Resend API key (for transaction receipts/invites). - TERMII_API_KEY: Your Termii API key (for SMS broadcasts). - PORT: 5000 (or leave default if the hosting provider assigns it automatically).

docs.title: 2. Backend Build Settings
docs.text:
• Build Command: npm install (run from the root)
  • Start Command: npx tsx server/src/index.ts (or build to JS and run node server/dist/index.js for production scale).

docs.title: 3. Deploying specifically to Fly.io
docs.text:
We configure Fly.io using the Dockerfile, .dockerignore, and fly.toml files at the root of the project: 1. Log in to the Fly CLI: 

fly auth login
2. Create the application: 
fly apps create remitpro-backend --org remitpro-ltd
3. Set your production secrets securely: 
fly secrets set DATABASE_URL="..." VITE_SUPABASE_URL="..." MONNIFY_API_KEY="..." MONNIFY_SECRET_KEY="..." MONNIFY_BASE_URL="https://api.monnify.com"
4. Deploy the application: 
fly deploy --remote-only

docs.title: 💻 Step 4: Frontend Deployment (Vercel)
docs.text:
1. Connect your GitHub repository to Vercel.
  2. Configure the Build & Development Settings:
  3. Build Command: npm run build
  4. Output Directory: dist
  5. Framework Preset: Vite
  6. Add the following Environment Variables in Vercel:
  7. VITE_SUPABASE_URL: Your Production Supabase URL.
  8. VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: Your Production Supabase Anon Key.
  9. VITE_MICROSERVICES_URL: The URL of your deployed Express backend server on Fly.io: https://remitpro-backend.fly.dev/api.
  10. Vercel automatically honors the vercel.json file in the root directory to handle SPA routing redirects correctly.

docs.title: 👮 Step 5: Platform Owner Initialization
docs.text:
Once the frontend and backend are live: 1. Navigate to your live site and Sign Up with your owner email. 2. Go to your local terminal and run the admin elevation script against the production DB: 

# Update OWNER_EMAIL in .env to match your signup email
npx tsx src/db/promote_admin.ts
3. Your account is now elevated to a platform-wide Super-Admin.

docs.title: 🛡️ Step 6: Post-Deployment Checklist
docs.text:
1. Webhook Configuration: In the Monnify Dashboard, set your live Webhook URL to point to the Express backend endpoint: https://remitpro-backend.fly.dev/api/monnify-webhook
  2. CORS Security: Ensure your Supabase project's Site URL and Redirect URLs match your production frontend domain.
  3. SSL: Ensure your frontend and Express backend are served exclusively over HTTPS.

docs.title: 📈 Monitoring & Maintenance
docs.text:
• System Logs: Inspect the logs of your Express backend server on Fly.io (e.g. using fly logs --app remitpro-backend in the CLI) for payment hook delivery and user invitation statuses.
  • Neon DB Health: Use the Neon console to monitor active connections, auto-scaling CPU usage, and slow query execution plans.
  • Database Backups: Configure automatic point-in-time recovery (PITR) directly in Neon console.

For critical infrastructure support, contact the Remitpro DevOps team.

docs.title: Remitpro: Deep Technical Manual 🛠️
docs.text: This document provides architects and developers with a deep-dive into the internal workings of the Remitpro platform.

docs.title: Logical Isolation (Row Level Security)
docs.text:
Remitpro uses a shared-database, shared-schema multi-tenant model. Isolation is strictly enforced at the database level using PostgreSQL Row Level Security (RLS) on Neon.

  • Global Context: The auth.uid() of the requester is used to fetch the school_id from the profiles table.
  • Enforcement: Most tables have an ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY command applied.
  • Policies: 
    CREATE POLICY school_isolation_policy ON students 
USING (school_id = (SELECT school_id FROM profiles WHERE id = auth.uid()));
  • Super-Admin Bypass: Super-admins have a distinct role in the profiles table that allows them to bypass RLS for platform-wide monitoring.

docs.title: 2. Database Schema (Drizzle ORM)
docs.text:
The schema is designed for high financial integrity and auditability. Key entities include:

  • Schools: Primary tenant entity. Stores Monnify sub-account codes and academic cycle settings.
  • Profiles: Extended user data. Links individual Auth users to a school.
  • Master Fee Schedule: The "Rulebook" for a school. Defines what each class (e.g., JSS1) should pay.
  • Student Ledgers: The "Debt Tracker". When a term starts, entries are generated here based on the Master Schedule.
  • Transactions: Immutable records of every Naira that enters the system.

docs.title: 💰 3. The "₦200 Flat-Fee" Revenue Model
docs.text: Remitpro implements a unique revenue model where the platform owner earns ₦200 on every transaction.

docs.title: Implementation Logic
docs.text:
The split is handled during the Monnify Transaction Initialization: 1. Request: The system requests a transaction for ₦10,000. 2. Split Rule: - Platform Subaccount: ₦200 (Fixed) - School Subaccount: ₦9,800 (Remainder) 3. Settlement: Monnify handles the split instantly upon payment.

Note: This is managed within the generate-virtual-account and monnify-webhook microservice routes.

docs.title: ⚡ 4. Microservices (Node/Express Server) & Integrations
docs.text: The backend backend services run on a Node/Express server (located in server/) written in TypeScript, connecting directly to the Neon PostgreSQL database using Drizzle ORM. Frontend invocations of supabase.functions.invoke(...) are intercepted in client.ts and redirected to http://localhost:5000/api/<function-name>.

docs.title: onboard-school
docs.text:
• Trigger: Called manually from the Onboarding UI.
  • Route: POST /api/onboard-school
  • Logic: Creates the schools record in Neon and asynchronously calls Monnify to provision a subAccountCode. Links the current user as the admin of that school.

docs.title: generate-virtual-account
docs.text:
• Trigger: Triggered when a staff member creates a virtual account for a student.
  • Route: POST /api/generate-virtual-account
  • Logic: Provisions a reserved account (Wema/Rolez) via Monnify. The account is permanently mapped to the student's studentId and saved to Neon.

docs.title: monnify-webhook
docs.text:
• Trigger: Incoming HTTP POST from Monnify.
  • Route: POST /api/monnify-webhook
  • Logic:
  • Validates the Monnify Hash Signature.
  • Identifies the student via the productCode or transactionReference.
  • Updates the student_ledgers directly in Neon (marking debts as paid).
  • Records the transactions entry for auditing.

docs.title: verify-payment
docs.text:
• Trigger: Client-side widget callback or manual admin check.
  • Route: POST /api/verify-payment
  • Logic: Queries Monnify API directly (Server-to-Server) to confirm status of a transaction reference. Updates the Neon DB directly only if valid. Sends receipt via send-email.

docs.title: invite-user
docs.text:
• Trigger: Admin sends invitation.
  • Route: POST /api/invite-user
  • Logic: Handles Supabase Auth invitation via the administrative SDK, assigns multi-tenant schoolId and role in Neon, and sends branded onboarding email via Resend.

docs.title: send-email
docs.text:
• Trigger: Internal Service Call (supabase.functions.invoke).
  • Route: POST /api/send-email
  • Logic: Proxies requests to Resend API. Keeps API keys hidden from client. Log-only mode if keys missing.

docs.title: promote-students
docs.text:
• Trigger: Admin "End Session" action.
  • Route: POST /api/promote-students
  • Logic:
  • Batches students by class.
  • Updates class string (e.g., JSS1 -> JSS2) in Neon.
  • Archives current academic records for historical reporting.
  • Resets currentTerm to TERM_1.

docs.title: 5.1 Resilience & Offline Sync
docs.text: The platform implements an Offline-First strategy for high-frequency operations: - Service Worker (PWA): Uses vite-plugin-pwa to cache assets and core UI, allowing the app to launch without internet. - Attendance Queueing: Uses OfflineStore (LocalStorage) to capture student roll-calls. - Background Sync: A useEffect hook monitors the online window event. Upon reconnection, it automatically upserts the local queue to Neon and clears the cache.

docs.title: 5.2 Biometric Clock-In Logic
docs.text:
• Geofencing: Utilizes geolib to compare the teacher's current coordinates against the school's configured geofence_lat/lng. Access to the camera is only granted if the distance is within the geofence_radius.
  • Liveness Verification: Implemented a multi-state machine for biometric capture, requiring the user to smile/blink to generate the storage-ready attendance photo.

docs.title: 5.3 Institutional Ecosystem Services
docs.text:
• CurriculumService: Manages the versioned repository of academic documents. Documents are stored in protected Supabase buckets with RLS metadata.
  • AnnouncementService: Centralized engine for class-based and school-wide broadcasts.
  • QuizService: Handles the logic for Computer Based Testing, including randomized question rendering and automated result computation.

docs.title: Indigo-Night Design System
docs.text: The platform utilizes a custom-built design system based on: - Primary Color: Modern Indigo (hsl(243 75% 59%)). - Surface Logic: Glassmorphism (backdrop-blur-xl) with high-transparency card backgrounds. - Dark Mode: Persisted using next-themes and the class attribute on the <html> element.

docs.title: State-of-the-Art Visuals
docs.text:
• Mesh Gradients: Used in the Login/Onboarding background for professional "Fintech" depth.
  • Dynamic Logo Inversion: The .brand-logo utility automatically inverts logo colors for dark backgrounds using CSS filters.

docs.title: 📈 7. Audit & Compliance
docs.text:
• Audit Logs: Sensitive actions (fee changes, manual payment records) are captured in the audit_logs table.
  • Immutability: Once a transaction is recorded, it cannot be deleted or modified through the application UI.

docs.title: 🛡️ 8. Type Safety & Error Handling
docs.text: Starting with v1.2, the platform enforces strict TypeScript conventions to eliminate "runtime surprises."

docs.title: 8.1 Specific Typing over any
docs.text:
• Data Entities: Components use Supabase-generated Tables types from @/integrations/supabase/types.
  • Service Methods: All service methods (e.g., PayrollService, StaffService) are strictly typed to ensure predictable data flow.
  • Mutations: React Query mutations use typeof formData or specific interfaces to validate inputs before submission.

docs.title: 8.2 Robust Error Boundaries
docs.text:
All asynchronous operations use a standardized error-catching pattern: 

try {
  // async operation
} catch (err: unknown) {
  const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : "A persistent error occurred";
  toast.error(message);
}
This ensures that the user always receives a human-readable explanation of failures, and the system never silently crashes due to malformed error objects.

docs.title: Manual Testing & QA Guide: Remitpro 🧪
docs.text: This guide provides a comprehensive framework for manual Quality Assurance (QA) to ensure the platform meets enterprise financial standards and multi-tenant security requirements.

docs.title: 📋 1. Testing Prerequisites
docs.text: Before starting, ensure you have: - Access to a Neon development database. - Access to a Supabase project (for Auth/Storage). - A Monnify Sandbox account (for API testing). - Local Express backend running via npm run server:dev (on port 5000). - Local React development server running via npm run dev. - Clean database state (no existing schools/students).

docs.title: Session A: Self-Onboarding & Identity
docs.text:
Objective: Verify a new school can enter the ecosystem and provision their workspace.

Task ID Task Description Expected Result A1 Register a new account with valid institutional details. User is redirected to /onboarding automatically. A2 Complete Step 1 (Profile) and Step 2 (Bank Details). Workspace is created; Monnify sub-account code is generated in DB. A3 Attempt to bypass Onboarding by visiting / directly after signup. AuthGuard intercepts and redirects back to /onboarding. A4 Logout and Login again. Dashboard loads instantly for the provisioned workspace.

docs.title: Session B: Student & Fee Management
docs.text:
Objective: Verify the "Finance Core" can handle institutional rules.

Task ID Task Description Expected Result B1 Create a "Tuition Fees" category for the current term. Category appears in the Fee Structure list. B2 Add a mandatory fee for "JSS1" class (₦50,000). The fee is saved to the master_fee_schedule with correct frequency. B3 Bulk Import 10 students via CSV. All students appear in directory with correct metadata (Class, Enrollment ID). B4 Verify "Initial Debt": Check a student ledger after import. Student should automatically have the ₦50,000 debt mapped to their term.

docs.title: Session C: Virtual Accounts & Payments
docs.text:
Objective: Test the "Fintech Engine" (Sandbox Mode).

Task ID Task Description Expected Result C1 Click "Generate Virtual Account" for a student. System calls Monnify; displays a bank name (Wema) and account number. C2 Mock a Payment: Use the Monnify Sandbox to simulate a transfer. Webhook is triggered; Transaction entry is created in Neon DB. C3 Verify Debt Clearance: Re-check student ledger. Student's isPaid status changes to true instantly. C4 Partial Payment Test: Simulate a payment of ₦20,000 for ₦50,000 debt. Ledger shows ₦30,000 outstanding; status is "Partial".

docs.title: Session D: Multi-Tenant Isolation (SECURITY CRITICAL)
docs.text:
Objective: Ensure School A cannot see School B's data.

Task ID Task Description Expected Result D1 Create School A and Student X. Record created successfully. D2 Open a new incognito window, create School B. School B Dashboard should show 0 students (Student X hidden). D3 Attempt to access Student X's ID directly via URL in School B's session. System returns 404 or "Access Denied" via RLS.

docs.title: Session E: Theme & Visual Consistency
docs.text:
Objective: Verify the "Indigo-Night" design system integrity.

Task ID Task Description Expected Result E1 Toggle Dark Mode on the Login Page. Background orbs dim; card becomes high-contrast; logo inverts to white. E2 Navigate through the Dashboard in Light Mode. Glassmorphism blur is crisp; shadows feel soft and diffused. E3 Test Sidebar Responsiveness. Sidebar transitions smoothly between mobile (drawer) and desktop (fixed).

docs.title: 📈 3. QA Reporting Template
docs.text:
When documenting a bug, use the following format:

Title: [Module] - Brief Description Severity: [P1 - Critical / P2 - Major / P3 - Cosmetic] Steps: 1. ... 2. ... Expected: What should happen. Actual: What actually happened (Attach screenshot if visual). Environment: [Browser / Screen Size / Theme]

docs.title: Security & Compliance Framework 🛡️
docs.text: Remitpro is engineered with a "Security-First" philosophy, ensuring that institutional data and financial transactions are protected at every layer.

docs.title: Row Level Security (RLS)
docs.text: The core of our multi-tenant security is PostgreSQL Row Level Security. - Tenant Isolation: Every database query is scoped to a specific school_id. It is mathematically impossible for a user from School A to view or modify data from School B. - JWT Verification: Supabase Auth tokens carry the user's identity, which is verified by the database against the profiles table before any data is returned.

docs.title: Encryption
docs.text:
• In Transit: All data is transmitted via TLS 1.2+ (SSL) encryption.
  • At Rest: Database volumes and file storage (Supabase Bucket) are encrypted using AES-256.
  • PII Protection: Personally Identifiable Information (Names, Phone Numbers) is stored in a hardened database environment.

docs.title: Monnify Integration
docs.text: Remitpro does not store raw credit card data or bank login credentials. - Sub-account Privacy: Each school's financial environment is isolated within Monnify's PCI-DSS Level 1 compliant infrastructure. - Webhook Authentication: Every incoming payment notification is validated using a SHA-512 HMAC Signature. Notifications that do not pass the signature check are instantly rejected.

docs.title: Biometric & Geofence Security
docs.text:
• Physical Verification: Staff clock-ins are restricted using GPS Geofencing. Unauthorized remote clock-ins are blocked at the application layer.
  • Liveness Checking: Our facial capture system requires proof of liveness (randomized user actions like smiling/blinking), preventing "photo-of-a-photo" spoofing.
  • Storage Privacy: Attendance photos and biometric data are stored in non-public Supabase buckets with strict role-based access.

docs.title: Fraud Prevention
docs.text:
• Unique Transaction Refs: Every payment request generates a unique UUID to prevent double-spending or replay attacks.
  • Settlement Controls: Funds are settled directly to the bank account verified during onboarding, preventing unauthorized rerouting of school revenue.

docs.title: System Audit Trails
docs.text: Sensitive administrative actions are logged in the audit_logs table: - Changes to Fee Structures. - Manual payment reconciliations. - Staff role elevations. - Security-critical configuration changes.

docs.title: Access Control (RBAC)
docs.text: We enforce strictly defined roles: - Super-Admin: Managed via server-side elevation only (Platform Oversight). - School Admin: Full control over a single school workspace. - Bursar/Staff: Limited access to student management and reporting.

docs.title: Resilience & Offline Security
docs.text:
• Secure Offline Storage: Data cached for offline attendance is stored in browser-encrypted zones.
  • Automatic Sanitization: Cached data is automatically flushed once a background sync to the primary server is successful.
  • Fintech Robustness: Backend microservices (like VA generation) implement administrative/service-account failover. If a user session is transiently invalid, the system securely completes critical financial provisioning using hardened backend credentials to prevent workflow interruption.
  • Strict Validation: All incoming data, whether from an offline sync or direct API, is validated against strict Zod schemas to prevent payload injection or schema drift.

docs.title: 🏛️ 4. Compliance Standards
docs.text:
Remitpro is designed to help your institution meet local and global regulatory requirements: - NDPR: Nigerian Data Protection Regulation compliant. - PCI-DSS: Leveraging Monnify's certified payment gateway. - KYC: Automated Business Verification (BVN/CAC) via the onboarding flow.

Security Manual v1.0 | Prepared by Remitpro Security Team

docs.title: Service Level Agreement (SLA) 📜
docs.text: This SLA describes the service commitments and support levels for the Remitpro platform.

docs.title: 1. Uptime Commitment
docs.text: The platform aims for 99.9% availability during school business hours (07:00 – 18:00 WAT). - Core Systems: Auth, Dashboard, and Payment Processing. - Maintenance Windows: Scheduled maintenance occurs on weekends between 02:00 and 04:00 WAT, with 48-hour prior notice.

docs.title: 2. Support Levels
docs.text: Priority Description Target Response Target Resolution P1 - Critical System-wide outage; payments failing. 1 Hour 4 Hours P2 - High School-specific issue; student data inaccessible. 4 Hours 12 Hours P3 - Medium Minor bugs; reporting styling issues. 12 Hours 48 Hours P4 - Low Feature requests; documentation clarity. 48 Hours Next Release

docs.title: 3. Financial Settlements
docs.text:
• Settlement Window: Monnify funds are settled to the school's bank account on a T+1 basis (Next business day).
  • Platform Fee: The ₦200 platform fee is deducted at source during the transaction initialization.
  • Reporting Delay: Dashboard metrics reflect successful payments within 60 seconds of Monnify webhook confirmation.

docs.title: 4. Disaster Recovery
docs.text:
• Data Backup: Point-in-Time Recovery (PITR) with backups every 60 minutes.
  • Business Continuity: Multi-region database replicas in the event of a regional cloud provider failure.

docs.title: 5. Contact Channels
docs.text:
• Email: support@remitpro.io
  • Admin Hotline: Available for Enterprise Tier subscribers.

SLA v1.2 | Effective as of January 2026

docs.title: Remitpro: Institutional User Guide 🏫
docs.text: Welcome to Remitpro! This guide walks you through the lifecycle of managing your school's finances on our platform.

docs.title: 🚀 1. Getting Started: Onboarding
docs.text:
Once you sign up, you will be guided through a 2-step setup: 1. School Profile: Enter your school's official name, address, and support hotline. 2. Settlement Bank: Link your school's bank account (NUBAN). This is where all collected fees will be settled automatically by Monnify.

Result: Your "Workspace" becomes active, and a dedicated Monnify sub-account is created for you.

docs.title: 👥 2. Student Management
docs.text: To collect fees, you first need students in the system. - Bulk Import: Use the "Import Students" feature to upload your class lists via CSV. - Individual Enrollment: Add students manually through the "Students" directory.

docs.title: 💰 3. Setting Up Fees (The Master Schedule)
docs.text: Before parents can pay, you must define your fee structure. - Go to Finance > Fee Structure. - Define fees by class level (e.g., JSS1 Tuition, SS2 Lab Fees). - Specify whether a fee is Mandatory or Optional.

docs.title: Step 1: Virtual Account Generation
docs.text: Every student needs a dedicated bank account for payment. 1. Go to the Virtual Accounts page. 2. Select a student and click "Generate Account". 3. A unique account number (e.g., Wema Bank) is assigned to that student for life.

docs.title: Step 2: Parent Payment
docs.text: Parents pay by making a standard bank transfer to their child's unique account number. They don't need to log in!

docs.title: Step 3: Instant Reconciliation
docs.text: As soon as the transfer is made, Remitpro receives a notification. - The student's debt is automatically cleared. - You receive an instant update in your Revenue Summary.

docs.title: 📊 5. Monitoring & Reporting
docs.text:
• Revenue Summary: A class-by-class breakdown of what has been paid vs. what is outstanding.
  • Transaction History: A searchable log of every single payment.
  • Debt Lists: Generate a list of students with outstanding balances for your staff to follow up.

docs.title: 🌗 6. Personalizing Your Dashboard
docs.text:
• Dark Mode: Toggle the Moon/Sun icon in the top right for a more comfortable viewing experience at night.
  • Academic Cycle: Update your current Session and Term in settings to ensure payments are credited to the correct period.
  1. Curriculum Oversight:
    • Review Lesson Plans and Schemes of Work uploaded by teachers.
    • Click "Approve" to make them part of the official session repository.

docs.title: Broadcast Center
docs.text: Communicate with your community instantly: 1. Go to Communication > Broadcast Center. 2. Select your audience (e.g., "All Parents"). 3. Type your message and choose to send via SMS, Email, or Parent Portal.

docs.title: Staff Clock-In
docs.text:
Maintain high operational standards: - Staff use their mobile phones to clock in. - The system verifies their GPS location and performs a facial liveness check. - You view proof of attendance instantly in your Staff Dashboard.

For support, contact your platform administrator or visit the Help Center.

docs.title: Parent Portal Access Guide 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦
docs.text: Welcome to the Remitpro Parent Portal! This guide explains how to access your dashboard, manage school fee payments, and view your child's academic performance.

docs.title: 🔐 1. Accessing the Portal
docs.text: The Parent Portal is designed for ease of access without complex passwords.

docs.title: Login Credentials
docs.text: To log in securely, you will need two pieces of information provided by the school: 1. Student ID: Your child's unique registration number (e.g., STU/102). 2. Parent Phone Number: The phone number registered with the school for communication.

docs.title: Steps to Login
docs.text:
1. Visit the school's Parent Portal URL (provided by your school administrator).
  2. Enter the Student ID and Registered Phone Number.
  3. Click "Access Portal".

Note: If you have multiple children in the school, you will need to log in separately for each child using their unique Student ID.

docs.title: 💳 2. Fee Payments Made Simple
docs.text: Remitpro uses a Virtual Account System to make fee payment as easy as a regular bank transfer.

docs.title: Your Unique Payment Account
docs.text: Upon admission, every student is assigned a dedicated Virtual Bank Account (e.g., Wema Bank - 1234567890). * Where to find it: This account number is displayed prominently at the top of your Portal Dashboard. * How it works: This account is permanent and attached solely to your child. Any money sent to this account is instantly credited to your child's school fee balance.

docs.title: How to Pay
docs.text:
1. Open your personal bank app (GTBank, Zenith, OPay, etc.).
  2. Make a transfer to the Virtual Account Number shown on your dashboard.
  3. That's it! The payment is automatically detected.
    • You will receive an instant digital receipt.
    • The "Outstanding Balance" on your dashboard will update immediately.

docs.title: 💰 Your Prepaid Wallet
docs.text: Instead of direct bank transfers for every small item, you can use your Prepaid Wallet: 1. Fund Wallet: Transfer money to your unique student account. 2. Instant Payments: Use your wallet balance to pay for school store items, excursion fees, or termly tuition with a single click. 3. Balance Tracking: View your current "Available Balance" at the top of the screen.

docs.title: 📊 3. Academic Results & Performance
docs.text: Stay updated on your child's progress without waiting for physical report cards.

docs.title: Viewing Grades
docs.text:
• Navigate to the "Academics" or "Results" tab.
  • Select the current Session (e.g., 2024/2025) and Term.
  • You will see a breakdown of:
    • Continuous Assessment (CA) scores.
    • Exam scores.
    • Total and Grade (A, B, C...).

docs.title: Behavioral Reports
docs.text: Beyond grades, view teacher assessments on your child's soft skills: * Punctuality * Neatness * Politeness * Handwriting

docs.title: Downloadable Report Cards
docs.text: At the end of the term, you can download the official School Term Report (PDF) directly from the portal for your records.

docs.title: 📜 4. Transaction History
docs.text: Keep track of every kobo spent on your child's education. * Click on "Payment History" to see a full statement of account. * View dates, amounts paid, and payment references for your records. * Check the specific fee items (Tuition, Bus Service, Uniforms) that your payments have covered.

docs.title: 📢 5. Stay Informed (School Broadcasts)
docs.text: Never miss a school announcement again: * Notice Board: View latest school news directly on your dashboard. * Alert Notifications: High-priority broadcasts are flagged at the top of your portal page. * Multi-channel Alerts: Receive official announcements via Email/SMS alongside the portal notice.

docs.title: ❓ Frequently Asked Questions
docs.text:
Q: Can I pay for two children at once? A: No. Please make separate transfers to each child's unique Virtual Account Number. This ensures the system knows exactly which student to credit.

Q: What happens if I overpay? A: Any excess amount remains in the student's wallet and will be automatically applied to the next term's fees.

Q: I forgot my login details. A: Please contact the school administrator to confirm the exact Student ID or Phone Number they have on file for you.

docs.title: School Administrator Reference Manual 🏫
docs.text: This manual serves as the comprehensive guide for School Administrators using Remitpro. It covers day-to-day operations, financial setup, and academic management.

docs.title: 👥 1. Student Boarding & Management
docs.text: Efficiently manage your student database.

docs.title: Adding New Students
docs.text:
1. Navigate to "Students" in the sidebar.
  2. Click "Add Student".
  3. Fill in the required details:
    • Personal Info: First/Last Name, Date of Birth.
    • Class Assignment: Select current Class Level (e.g., JSS 1).
    • Parent Contact: Critical for SMS alerts and Portal access.
  4. Save. A Virtual Account Number is automatically generated for the student immediately.

docs.title: Bulk Import (CSV)
docs.text: For setting up a new session or importing large lists: 1. Go to Students > Import. 2. Download the CSV Template. 3. Fill in the data (Do not change header columns). 4. Upload the file. The system will validate entries and create profiles in bulk.

docs.title: Step A: Configuring Fees (The Master Schedule)
docs.text: Before a term begins, you must tell the system what payments are expected. 1. Go to Finance > Fee Schedule. 2. Add Fee Item: * Category: Tuition, Uniform, Bus, etc. * Class: Apply to specific classes (e.g., "All JSS 1" or "JSS 1A"). * Amount: The cost in Naira. * Mandatory?: Toggle 'Yes' for compulsory fees (Tuition), 'No' for optional (Excursion). 3. The system will automatically apply these bills to all eligible students.

docs.title: Step B: Tracking Revenue
docs.text: Use the Dashboard for a real-time overview: * Total Expected: The sum of all bills generated for the term. * Total Collected: Actual money sitting in your settlement balance. * Outstanding: Money yet to be paid.

docs.title: Step C: Managing Debtors
docs.text:
1. Go to Finance > Debtors List.
  2. Filter by Class or Minimum Amount Owed.
  3. Action:
    • Send Reminder: Triggers an SMS/Email to the parent.
    • Export: Download the list for offline follow-up.

docs.title: Step D: Expense Tracking
docs.text: Keep a record of school spending (e.g., Diesel, repairs). 1. Go to Finance > Expenses. 2. Click "Log Expense". 3. Enter Description, Amount, Category, and Date. 4. Upload receipt image (optional) for auditing.

docs.title: Step E: Parent Wallets
docs.text: Remitpro features an internal digital wallet for every student. - Funding: Parents can fund the wallet via the Portal. - Settlement: Use the "Pay from Wallet" option in the Parent Portal or School Dashboard to settle fees, store items, or transport costs instantly without bank transfer delays.

docs.title: Setting Up Subjects
docs.text:
1. Go to Academics > Subjects.
  2. Create master subjects (e.g., Mathematics, Basic Science).
  3. Assign subjects to Classes (e.g., "Physics" for SS Classes only).

docs.title: Processing Results
docs.text:
1. Data Entry:
    • Teachers log in to enter CA (Continuous Assessment) and Exam scores.
    • Admins can bulk upload scores via CSV.
  2. Computation:
    • The system auto-calculates Totals and Grades based on your scale.
    • Class Positions (1st, 2nd, 3rd) are ranked automatically.
  3. Publishing:
    • Go to Academics > Sessions.
    • Click "Publish Results" to make them visible on the Parent Portal.

docs.title: Curriculum Repository
docs.text: Centralize your school's academic assets: 1. Teachers upload Lesson Plans and Schemes of Work. 2. Admins review and click "Approve". 3. Approved documents form the master repository for the session, ensuring academic quality.

docs.title: Computer Based Testing (CBT)
docs.text:
1. Build Quiz: Teachers create timed quizzes with multiple-choice questions.
  2. Assign: link the quiz to a specific Subject and Class.
  3. Monitor: View real-time scores as students complete assessments.

docs.title: 📦 4. Inventory & Store (Uniforms/Books)
docs.text: Manage non-tuition items sold by the school.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Go to Store > Inventory.
  2. Add Item: Name (e.g., "JSS Math Text"), Price, and Initial Stock Quantity.

docs.title: Recording Sales
docs.text: When a parent purchases an item: 1. Go to Store > New Sale. 2. Select the Student. 3. Add items to cart. 4. Checkout: * If paid via transfer to Virtual Account, the wallet balance is deducted. * Inventory count is automatically updated.

docs.title: Session Management
docs.text:
• New Term: When a term ends, go to Settings > Academic Session. Select "Start New Term". This archives current records and prepares the system for new fees/results.
  • Promote Students: At the end of a session, use the Batch Promotion tool to move students (e.g., JSS1 -> JSS2).

docs.title: Staff Access
docs.text:
1. Go to Settings > Staff Management.
  2. Invite staff via email.
  3. Roles:
    • Teacher: Can only view their assigned class and enter grades.
    • Bursar: Can view finance/store but cannot alter academic records.
    • Admin: Full access.

docs.title: Broadcast Center
docs.text: Send urgent notices or routine announcements: 1. Go to Communication > Broadcast Center. 2. Filter audience: Everyone, Parents, or Staff. 3. Choose channel: SMS, Email, or In-Portal. 4. Type message and Send.

docs.title: Staff Clock-In (HR Compliance)
docs.text: Ensure your staff is on-site: - Setup: Define your school's GPS coordinates in Settings. - Usage: Staff use the "Mobile Clock-In" page on their phones. The system verifies they are within the school radius and captures a facial photo with liveness check before allowing the clock-in. - Oversight: View the daily verification map and photos in Staff > Attendance.

docs.title: Appraisals
docs.text:
1. Setup termly objectives and KPIs for your staff.
  2. Conduct reviews at the end of the term.
  3. Track performance history to identify top educators.

For technical issues, please refer to the TECHNICAL_MANUAL.md or contact the Platform Super Admin.

docs.title: Super-Admin Setup & User Guide 👮‍♂️
docs.text: This guide explains how to initialize the platform owner account and manage the Remitpro SaaS ecosystem.

docs.title: 🛠️ Step 1: Initial Setup (The "God Mode" Promotion)
docs.text: For security reasons, no account can register as a Super-Admin via the UI. You must manually promote your account using the provided database script.

docs.title: 1. Register an Account
docs.text: Sign up normally through the application's login/signup page with your official email (e.g., admin@remitpro.io).

docs.title: 2. Configure the Promotion Script
docs.text:
Open src/db/promote_admin.ts and update the OWNER_EMAIL variable with the email you just registered:

const OWNER_EMAIL = 'your-email@example.com';

docs.title: 3. Run the Promotion
docs.text:
Execute the script using tsx (ensure your .env is configured with the DATABASE_URL):

npx tsx src/db/promote_admin.ts

docs.title: 4. Verify Access
docs.text: Refresh your browser. You will now see a "Platform Admin" section in the sidebar with a "SaaS Platform" link.

docs.title: 📊 Step 2: The Super-Admin Dashboard
docs.text: Located at /superadmin, this dashboard is your primary command center for the entire platform.

docs.title: Key Metrics (KPIs)
docs.text:
• Total Onboarded Schools: Count of every individual institution using your platform.
  • Total Platform Revenue: The real-time sum of the ₦200 platform fee collected from every successful student payment across all schools.
  • Active Students Managed: Total enrollment across the entire ecosystem.

docs.title: Remittance Intelligence Hub
docs.text: The dashboard now features deep financial transparency for the SaaS owner: - Projected Revenue: Based on active student accounts and termly fee cycles. - Verified Settlements: Track real-time fund splitting across the Monnify stack. - School Health Index: Monitor which schools have highest collection rates.

docs.title: 💰 Step 3: Managing the "₦200 Rule"
docs.text: The platform is configured to automatically split payments.

docs.title: How Revenue is Collected
docs.text:
1. When a Student pays, Monnify uses the incomeSplitConfig we provided during setup.
  2. The School receives theirs minus processing fees.
  3. Your Platform Share (₦200) is logically tracked in the transactions.platform_fee column.

docs.title: Maintenance
docs.text: To ensure your revenue is settled correctly to your own bank account: - Ensure you have a Main Merchant Account on Monnify. - Check that the PLATFORM_SUBACCOUNT_CODE environment variable is set in your Supabase secrets.

docs.title: Pending Subaccounts
docs.text: If a school shows "PENDING" in the Subaccount column: 1. They may have entered incorrect bank account details. 2. You can manually re-trigger onboarding or update their record in the schools table via Supabase Dashboard once valid details are provided.

docs.title: Isolation Verification
docs.text:
If you ever need to verify that Multi-Tenancy (RLS) is working: 1. Log in as a School Admin. 2. Try to navigate to /superadmin. The application will either hide the link or the database will return 0 results because of Row Level Security.

This guide is for internal use by Remitpro Platform Developers only.
